opening_message: I need the ClearGrip case for my Pixel 7, but do not add it until you verify why the Pixel 8 one is not the right fit.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You need a ClearGrip case for your Pixel 7, but you noticed there is also a Pixel 8 version and you do not want the wrong one added. You want the agent to verify the compatibility difference first, then ask before adding the Pixel 7 case.
known_info: You know your current request and how you asked for it.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
Which near-match products or variants actually fit the intended device or preference.
task_rules: If the agent distinguishes the Pixel 7 and Pixel 8 ClearGrip cases using catalog or compatibility evidence, agree that the Pixel 7 case is the one you want.
If the agent asks to add the Pixel 7 case after explaining why the Pixel 8 case is wrong for your phone, consent to the add.
If the agent tries to add the Pixel 8 case, an iPhone case, or any case before verifying compatibility, object once that you asked for the Pixel 7 fit to be checked first.
If the agent asks about shipping, points, promos, or partner devices, keep the request scoped to the Pixel 7 case only.
When the Pixel 7 case has been added, no wrong case was added, and the agent gives a cart summary, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_004 opens with: "I need the ClearGrip case for my Pixel 7, but do not add it until you verify why the Pixel 8 one is not the right fit." Relevant catalog/policy surface: ClearGrip Case for Pixel 7 (SP-H107), ClearGrip Case for Pixel 8 (SP-H108), ClearGrip Slim Case for iPhone 14 (SP-H109). Correct final state: Explain Pixel 7 vs Pixel 8 compatibility, get consent, then add only the Pixel 7 case.

**Challenge:** Near-duplicate cases require compatibility verification and a consent gate before the add. Primary pitfall: Adds immediately, skips compatibility evidence, or adds the Pixel 8 case. Correct behavior: Explain Pixel 7 vs Pixel 8 compatibility, get consent, then add only the Pixel 7 case. Novelty: Different from existing compatibility tasks because the user names the device and two near-duplicate case generations are both plausible. Family: compatibility_variant.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent explained from catalog/compatibility evidence why the Pixel 8 case is not the right fit for Pixel 7.
Agent got customer consent after the compatibility explanation and before adding the Pixel 7 case.
Agent added ClearGrip Case for Pixel 7 and did not add Pixel 8 or iPhone case.
Agent did not call unrelated write tools such as redeem_loyalty_points or set_shipping_option unless the customer explicitly requested that workflow for this task.
